qualifying_org_definition: Any public or private nonprofit organization (examples include churches, senior centers, town offices, food shelves). The participant is responsible for identifying their own volunteer site; no pre-approved list or state-assigned placement is required. For the standalone workfare path (outside ICAN/E&T), no worksite agreement between the nonprofit and the state or an ICAN provider is required. Note: when workfare is delivered as an ICAN E&T component, the ICAN provider must execute a formal worksite agreement with the volunteer site — but this is the structured E&T track, not the general nonprofit self-report lane Tended would use.
verification_instrument: Form 218-WFV (Workfare Verification Form) — completed and signed by the nonprofit supervisor after hours are performed; submitted by participant to Vermont Economic Services Division (ESD). For participants using ICAN to meet the TLBWR, ESD Form 218EPV (Employment Program Verification) is used instead.
hours_formula_detail: Household 3SquaresVT benefit amount ÷ Vermont minimum wage ($14.42/hr in 2026). This produces a personalized monthly hour target communicated to the participant at approval. A single ABAWD at maximum allotment (~$292/mo) yields ~20 hours. The 80-hour federal baseline does NOT apply to the workfare path in Vermont.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Form 218-WFV (Sept 2024 revision) asks only 'Is your organization a non-profit?' with a yes/no checkbox — no geographic location requirement is stated. The form FAQ says participants may volunteer at 'any non-profit public or private organization' with no in-state restriction. No DCF policy document reviewed imposed an in-state requirement. Under federal default (7 CFR §273.24), any qualifying nonprofit is permissible absent a state restriction. Operationally, a CA-based 501(c)(3) signing the 218-WFV should be accepted, but VT-ESD has not explicitly confirmed this.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Vermont previously pledged broad ABAWD service but under post-OBBBA rules the old broad waivers are eliminated. No Vermont county or region appears on FNA's current ABAWD waiver list for FY2025-2029 at the tightened ≥10% unemployment threshold. The record's abawd_enforced_2026=yes reflects full statewide enforcement.
submission_deadline: Submit after completing the first month of volunteering (initial verification), then again at next recertification interview. No specific calendar deadline (e.g., 10th of month) found in policy documents — submission is event-triggered.
notes:
Vermont calls its volunteer option 'workfare' but the lane is self-directed: participants identify their own nonprofit site (no state/county placement required, no MOU between nonprofit and state found in sources). The nonprofit supervisor signs Form 218-WFV which the participant submits to ESD. hours_reporting is set to county_determined because Vermont calculates a personalized monthly hour target (SNAP benefit ÷ Vermont minimum wage) rather than a flat 80 hours — ESD communicates this number to the participant — but no mandatory enrollment prestep blocks access. Confidence is medium because dcf.vermont.gov returned 403 errors, so primary state policy manual text could not be directly verified; findings rest on Vermont Foodbank (2026), legal-aid sources, and the SomeDoing state aggregator.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial research substantially resolved all material gaps and upgraded confidence to high. Primary source read: Vermont DCF-ESD ICAN Provider Handbook FFY27 (May 2026), a 34-page official policy document at outside.vermont.gov/dept/DCF/Shared Documents/ESD/Partners/ICAN-Provider-Handbook.pdf. Key adversarial finding investigated: the Workfare row in the handbook's component table (p. 24) states 'Volunteer sites required formal worksite agreements with the provider.' This initially appeared to be a hidden site-registration requirement. However, this language applies specifically to the ICAN/SNAP E&T workfare component, where a contracted ICAN provider agency places participants and must execute a worksite agreement with the nonprofit host. It does NOT apply to the standalone workfare path (7 CFR §273.24), which runs outside ICAN — in that path, the participant self-identifies their nonprofit and the nonprofit supervisor simply signs Form 218-WFV. The handbook (p. 32-33) explicitly lists standalone workfare as a separate TLBWR compliance option: 'Participating in Workfare (Volunteering for a set number of hours monthly. Hours are based on the household's 3SquaresVT benefit amount divided by Vermont's minimum wages)' — distinct from 'Participating in a qualifying component of ICAN.' Vermont Food Help (vermontfoodhelp.com/work-reporting-requirements) confirms the participant is responsible for identifying the nonprofit and the nonprofit completes the verification form with no state registration prerequisite described. No evidence of an approved site list, state MOU requirement for the nonprofit, or mandatory EDP/case-manager-meeting prestep was found for the standalone lane. ICAN enrollment itself is confirmed voluntary for most participants (handbook p. 8: 'The program is voluntary for most participants'). hours_reporting corrected from county_determined to self_reported: the participant submits hours documentation (Form 218-WFV) to ESD; the monthly hour TARGET is calculated by ESD (benefit ÷ VT minimum wage) and communicated to the participant, but this is not a caseworker-assignment mechanism — it is a formula disclosed at application. Vermont has no county structure, so county_determined was a misclassification. remote_stance set to silent: no language found either permitting or prohibiting remote volunteer work in the standalone workfare path. Tier 1 classification is confirmed. The ICAN worksite-agreement requirement applies only to the structured E&T placement track, not to the general nonprofit self-report lane that Tended would use.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Five new dimension groups added by reading Form 218-WFV PDF directly (outside.vermont.gov/dept/DCF/Shared%20Documents/ESD/Forms/218-WFV.pdf, revised Sept 2024). (1) HOURS FORMULA: Vermont explicitly uses the workfare formula — SNAP benefit ÷ Vermont minimum wage — NOT the federal 80-hour flat. VT minimum wage is $14.42/hr as of January 2026. A single ABAWD at max allotment (~$292/mo) yields ~20 hours/month. monthly_hours_required=20 is a representative figure; actual hours vary by benefit amount. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: Form 218-WFV asks only 'Is your organization a non-profit?' (yes/no) with no geographic restriction. Policy language says 'any non-profit public or private organization.' Classified as 'silent' — no explicit bar found, but VT-ESD has not explicitly confirmed acceptance of out-of-state 501(c)(3)s. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: None found. Vermont was not listed on FNA's FY2025-2029 ABAWD waiver table; under OBBBA the prior broad waivers are eliminated and no VT county meets the new ≥10% unemployment threshold required for a waiver. (4) SUBMISSION UX: Form 218-WFV FAQ instructs participants to 'submit the completed form to Economic Services' and directs them to 'your local Economic Services office or call 1-800-479-6151' — paper/in-person submission at county ESD office. Reporting cadence is at_recertification (submit after first month of meeting the requirement, then again at the next recertification interview). No specific calendar cutoff date (e.g., 10th of following month) was found. (5) FORM FIELDS: Read directly from 218-WFV PDF — recipient name, last-4 SSN, recipient phone, number of hours required; org name, org address, org phone, supervisor name, printed name, signature, date; is-nonprofit checkbox; and a month-by-month table of hours volunteered (hours_by_day_or_week mapped to this monthly table). EIN and notarization are NOT required. org_EIN not on form.
